decks.notes: The main idea of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought with Stifle, allowing the player to cheat a powerful creature into play for only 1 mana. The deck also includes card draw and countermagic to help protect and find the combo pieces. With a focus on speed and efficiency, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up its own game-winning combo.

The deck revolves around utilizing the combo of Replenish and Opalescence to bring back enchantments from the graveyard and turn them into creatures. Cards like Parallax Wave, Decree of Silence, and Seal of Cleansing are used to control the board while also setting up the combo. Attunement and Intuition help to dig for key pieces of the combo, while Tsabo's Web disrupts the opponent's mana base. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a board full of powerful enchantment creatures.

This deck focuses on quickly dealing damage to the opponent through efficient and aggressive burn spells and low-cost creatures. By building up a board presence and continuously pushing for damage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. With a mix of direct damage spells and fast creatures, this deck aims to pressure the opponent and end the game before it reaches the late game stages.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Madness mechanic to quickly play powerful creatures for cheap or for free. By discarding cards like Basking Rootwalla and Arrogant Wurm, the player can overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Savannah Lions and Wild Mongrel. With cards like Roar of the Wurm and Call of the Herd, the player can create additional threats on the battlefield. The deck also includes removal spells like Swords to Plowshares to deal with opposing threats, while Mishra's Factory and Wasteland help control the opponent's mana base.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells, removal spells, and land destruction. The deck aims to prevent the opponent from developing their board while also generating card advantage through cards like Fact or Fiction. The win condition is typically through resolving a Standstill, which allows the player to draw three cards while forcing the opponent to make a decision that can benefit the player. Overall, the deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan and outvaluing them in the long run.

This deck focuses on fast and aggressive gameplay, overwhelming the opponent with efficient and low-cost creatures and burn spells. By maintaining constant pressure and burning the opponent's life total, the deck aims to win the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize or mount a defense. The strategy revolves around dealing damage as quickly as possible, using creatures like Jackal Pup and Mogg Fanatic to attack early and efficiently, while burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast finish off the opponent's life total. The deck's goal is to win the game before the opponent can establish control or gain momentum.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using low-cost burn spells and efficient creatures. By utilizing fast mana sources and cheap, powerful spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast, the deck aims to maintain pressure and force the opponent to react quickly or risk losing the game. Grim Lavamancer provides additional reach and utility while cards like Sulfuric Vortex ensure a steady stream of damage if the game goes long. Overall, the deck is designed to end the game as quickly as possible through consistent, impactful damage output.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and land destruction to disrupt your opponent's strategy. It also aims to stall the game with cards like Standstill while setting up win conditions with creatures like Exalted Angel and Mishra's Factory. Additionally, the deck utilizes Faerie Conclave and Weathered Wayfarer to help with mana fixing and card advantage. Overall, the deck focuses on maintaining board control and outlasting the opponent to eventually secure a victory through consistent and calculated plays.

The key strategy of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's resources through land destruction effects like Stone Rain and Thermokarst while generating mana with cards like Mox Diamond and Rishadan Port. The deck aims to control the game with Sphere of Resistance and Oath of Druids, eventually winning by summoning powerful creature Terravore. The deck also includes card draw with Sylvan Library and removal with Lightning Bolt and Pyroclasm to maintain control on the battlefield.

The deck focuses on aggressive gameplay through the use of creatures like Terravore and Exalted Angel, while also disrupting the opponent's resources with cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port. The inclusion of Armageddon helps to further disrupt the opponent's mana base and maintain board control. The deck also features land destruction spells like Naturalize and Ray of Revelation to deal with any problematic enchantments or artifacts. Overall, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent while keeping them on the back foot with land destruction and disruption.

The deck focuses on utilizing Hermit Druid to put a large number of creatures into the graveyard quickly. This activates various combos involving cards like Animate Dead, Karmic Guide, and Triskelion to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress to disrupt the opponent's game plan and ensure its own combo can go off smoothly. The goal is to set up a strong board presence and win the game in a single explosive turn.

The deck focuses on dismantling its opponent's hand and board with discard spells like Duress and Cabal Therapy, while also controlling the battlefield through removal spells like Chainer's Edict and Smother. It aims to outvalue the opponent by generating card advantage with cards like Wall of Roots and Wall of Blossoms, and eventually closing out the game with powerful threats like Ravenous Baloth and Treetop Village. Additionally, it has access to versatile removal in the form of Pernicious Deed, providing flexibility in dealing with a wide range of threats.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful win condition. By using counterspells and card draw spells to protect and find the combo pieces, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent while setting up a game-winning play. The inclusion of cards like Foil and Daze allow for additional interaction and protection, making it crucial to carefully manage resources and timing. In a format where speed and efficiency are key, this deck excels at creating a potent threat that can quickly turn the tide in its favor.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through counterspells and discard spells while also utilizing the powerful creature Psychatog to pressure the opponent. The deck aims to efficiently draw cards using cantrips like Opt and Impulse, as well as card draw spells like Accumulated Knowledge and Fact or Fiction. By disrupting the opponent's game plan with counterspells like Counterspell and Duress, and removing threats with cards like Smother and Vendetta, the deck sets up Psychatog to close out the game quickly.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently assemble the Ill-Gotten Gains combo, utilizing cards like Lion's Eye Diamond, Dark Ritual, and Intuition to generate mana and card advantage. The combo involves using Ill-Gotten Gains to return key spells to the hand while also disrupting the opponent's graveyard, ultimately leading to a Brain Freeze win condition. The deck also includes various card draw and filtering tools to help find combo pieces and maintain consistency throughout the game.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle combo to disrupt opponents' strategies and win the game. It uses a combination of countermagic, card draw, and manipulation to find the necessary pieces and protect the combo until it can be executed. The deck also includes cards like Portent and Peek to further dig through the deck and set up the combo. Overall, the strategy revolves around efficiently assembling and protecting the key combo pieces to secure victory.
